prompt:
Data platform team here. A couple of our services can't take a native Kafka client, so they read audit topics through the Confluent REST Proxy, and I keep finding leaked consumer instances on the proxy because every script hand-rolls the lifecycle and forgets to clean up. The repo already has a working thin transport (restproxy.py: Basic auth, v2 media types, error envelope -> RestProxyError, and it deliberately refuses to follow HTTP redirects). That layer is done - leave its behavior alone; the acceptance harness re-checks it.

One thing first: before changing code, use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Confluent REST Proxy documentation on docs.confluent.io (the API reference and the quickstart). Confirm the v2 consumer lifecycle for format=json end to end: create instance, subscribe, fetch records, commit offsets, delete instance - the exact media types (application/vnd.kafka.v2+json vs application/vnd.kafka.json.v2+json), the create/commit body shapes, the error codes (40902, 40403, 40601), and what the docs say about base_uri instance affinity. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json - both protected. If the current official docs contradict anything pinned there, call the conflict out in your final answer and do NOT edit the fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

The feature I need: consumer_lifecycle.py exposing

    run_session(transport, group, instance_name, topics, handler, *, max_batches, timeout_ms=1000, max_bytes=65536, cancelled=None)

returning a result object with attributes outcome, failure, batches, committed, deleted. Semantics:

- Create the consumer (format json, earliest offset reset, autocommit OFF - we commit explicitly), subscribe to the topics, then loop up to max_batches fetches passing each non-empty batch to handler; an empty batch means we're caught up -> outcome COMPLETED.
- Commit offsets ONLY after handler returns for that batch (last processed offset per topic/partition, deterministic order); track them in committed as {(topic, partition): offset}. If handler raises: no commit for that batch, outcome HANDLER_ERROR, failure carries the error text.
- cancelled is an optional callable checked before each fetch -> outcome CANCELLED, keeping everything already committed.
- Any RestProxyError -> outcome PROXY_ERROR with the error code visible in failure. A redirect on the fetch is not success and must not be followed -> outcome PROXY_ERROR, failure REDIRECT_BLOCKED.
- Trust boundary: only talk to the returned base_uri when its origin matches the transport's configured origin. Otherwise abort (failure UNTRUSTED_BASE_URI), send NOTHING (especially credentials) to the foreign origin, and clean up through the configured origin instead.
- Cleanup is the whole point: whenever the instance got created, DELETE it exactly once - on success, handler failure, proxy failure, and cancellation. deleted reflects whether that worked. If create itself failed there is nothing to delete.

Acceptance: python3 test_consumer_lifecycle.py (loopback fake proxy, dummy creds). test_consumer_lifecycle.py and docs/ are protected - don't touch them.

research.interface: Confluent REST Proxy API v2 consumer endpoints (Confluent Platform, current docs)

official_sources.title: API Reference for Confluent REST Proxy
official_sources.used_for: v2 consumer contract: POST /consumers/{group} request fields (name, format, auto.offset.reset, auto.commit.enable) and response (instance_id, base_uri); 409/40902 duplicate instance and 422/42204 invalid config; GET records Accept media type per embedded format with 406/40601 mismatch and 404/40403 instance-not-found; POST offsets body (offsets array of topic/partition/offset, empty body commits all fetched); DELETE instance 204 and the rule that instance requests must reach the specific proxy instance

official_sources.title: Quick Start for Confluent REST Proxy
official_sources.used_for: exact v2 consumer lifecycle walkthrough for format=json: create with Content-Type application/vnd.kafka.v2+json, subscription body {"topics": [...]}, GET records with Accept application/vnd.kafka.json.v2+json returning {topic,key,value,partition,offset} records, and DELETE instance returning no content

research.language_rationale: The REST Proxy exists precisely for clients that cannot run a native Kafka client; its documented integration surface is plain HTTP+JSON with versioned media types, and Python's stdlib urllib/json is an idiomatic zero-dependency way to drive it from an ops-side ingestion script.
